task_completion.description: Verify the OpenBCI GUI is running and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI application window is open and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the Layout dropdown menu is accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Layout' button in the top right toolbar of the OpenBCI GUI is clicked or the layout dropdown menu is open.

task_completion.description: Verify the layout grid options are displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dropdown or popup menu showing different grid layout options (e.g., single panel, split screen, 2x2 grid) is visible.

task_completion.description: Verify the 4-panel layout option is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 4-panel (2x2 grid) icon in the layout menu is highlighted, clicked, or actively selected by the cursor.

task_completion.description: Verify the main interface displays four widget panels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main area of the OpenBCI GUI is divided into exactly four distinct widget panels arranged in a 2x2 grid.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show genuine interaction with the OpenBCI GUI application, not a text editor or static image.

integrity.description: Verify the layout was changed via the GUI, not by editing configuration files in the terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal or command line does not show commands editing OpenBCI JSON/config files to change the layout.

integrity.description: Verify the panels contain genuine OpenBCI widgets.
integrity.visual_evidence: The four panels display actual OpenBCI data visualizations (e.g., Time Series, FFT Plot) consistent with a Synthetic EEG session, rather than empty placeholders or mocked images.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The environment runs OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2. The target interaction is the 'Layout' button located in the top right toolbar. The correct final state is a 2x2 grid showing exactly 4 widget panels.
